chapter_title.en: Chapter 13 – Construction of Masjid of the Prophet<sup>saww</sup>

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول أو حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (5/68)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول أو حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (5/68)
text: حَدَّثَنِي مُوسَى بْنُ أُكَيْلٍ عَنْ عَبْدِ الْأَعْلَى مَوْلَى آلِ سَامٍ قَالَ قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) كَمْ كَانَ مَسْجِدُ رَسُولِ اللَّهِ ( صلى الله عليه وآله ) قَالَ كَانَ ثَلَاثَةِ آلَافٍ وَ سِتَّمِائَةِ ذِرَاعٍ تَكْسِيراً .

en.hubeali: Ahmad Bin Idrees and someone else, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, from Ali Bin Ismail, from Muhammad Bin Amro Bin Saeed who said, ‘Musa Bin Akeyl narrated to me, from Abdul A’la, a slave of the family of Saam who said,
‘I said to Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup>, ‘How much was (the size of) the Masjid of Rasool-Allah<sup>saww</sup>?’ He<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘It was of three thousand and six hundred cubits squared’.
en.sarwar: 3. Ahmad ibn Idris and other people have narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from Ali ibn ’Isma‘il from Muhammad ibn ‘Amr ibn Sa‘id who has said that Musa ibn ‘Ukayl narrated from ‘Abd al-‘A’la’ from Mawla Ala Sam who has said the following: “I asked abu ‘Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, ‘What was the size of Masjid of the Messenger of Allah?’ He (the Imam) replied, ‘It was three thousand and six hundred square yards.’”
